required_skills: Cloud Platform Architecture on AWS, GCP, or Azure
Infrastructure as Code and Cloud Provisioning
Virtual Networking, VPC Design, and Subnet Architecture
Container Orchestration and Managed Kubernetes Services
Cloud Storage, Object Storage, and Data Lake Architecture
Auto-Scaling, Load Balancing, and High Availability Design
Cost Management and Cloud Resource Optimization
preferred_skills: Cloud Migration Strategy and Lift-and-Shift Execution
FinOps Practices and Reserved Instance Planning
Cloud Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity Planning
Data Residency and Regulatory Compliance in Cloud Environments
responsibilities: Design and provision scalable, highly available cloud architectures on AWS or GCP using Terraform and CloudFormation with full state management and compliance guardrails
Architect virtual private cloud networks including subnets, route tables, NAT gateways, VPN connections, and transit gateway configurations for secure multi-account topologies
Implement and govern identity and access management policies with least-privilege IAM roles, service accounts, and cross-account access patterns across cloud organizations
Deploy and manage managed Kubernetes clusters on EKS, GKE, or AKS with cluster autoscaling, node pool management, and integrated monitoring pipelines
Design serverless event-driven architectures using AWS Lambda, API Gateway, SQS, and SNS to handle variable workloads without managing underlying compute infrastructure
Enforce cloud security posture management by configuring Security Hub, Cloud Armor, or Defender for Cloud with automated remediation for policy violations
Conduct cloud cost optimization reviews by analyzing resource utilization, rightsizing compute instances, and implementing savings plans to reduce monthly spend by measurable targets
Build and maintain multi-region disaster recovery architectures with defined RTO and RPO targets, automated failover mechanisms, and quarterly DR drill validation
Migrate on-premises workloads to cloud-native equivalents by assessing application dependencies, defining migration waves, and executing lift-and-shift or re-platforming strategies
Establish cloud governance frameworks including tagging standards, budget alerts, service control policies, and organizational unit structures across multi-account cloud environments
